title: The Larry David Sandwich

jokes.text: You know what I... you know or an adult drawn to the nipple.
jokes.setup: Larry compares his draw to water to a baby drawn to nipple
jokes.punchline: Realizes adults are also drawn to nipples, awkward correction
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry awkward overshare moment - starts innocent then realizes the sexual implication. Perfect character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Why does a Jew need a ticket to go to temple, it's crazy?
jokes.setup: Larry discovers he needs tickets for High Holy Days
jokes.punchline: His incredulous reaction to needing tickets for his own religion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David observation - taking a commonly accepted practice and pointing out its absurdity. High character integration.

jokes.text: Look you can't get your index finger in there, what's the point?
jokes.setup: Larry examining a coffee cup with tiny handle
jokes.punchline: His frustration at the impractical design while struggling with it
jokes.explanation: Solid Larry minutiae observation but fairly standard material about impractical design.

jokes.text: I like watching you do that.
jokes.setup: Larry struggling with the tiny cup handle
jokes.punchline: Jeff's amused observation of Larry's frustration
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat - Jeff enjoying Larry's minor torment. Economical but not particularly inventive.

jokes.text: They named a sandwich after me!
jokes.setup: Jeff reveals Larry has a sandwich named after him
jokes.punchline: Larry's disproportionate excitement over this minor honor
jokes.explanation: Larry's childlike pride in trivial achievement is perfectly in character and sets up the episode's central plot.

jokes.text: That's a disgusting sandwich really.
jokes.setup: Larry learns his sandwich has whitefish, sable, capers, onions and cream cheese
jokes.punchline: Immediately dismisses his own supposedly honored sandwich
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - goes from excitement to disgust instantly. Great irony and economy, very quotable.

jokes.text: You bumped into me!
jokes.setup: Larry spills coffee on someone
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately blames the other person despite clearly being at fault
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry deflection - never admitting fault. Good character comedy but fairly predictable situation.

jokes.text: Call me Mr. Tibbs!
jokes.setup: Random line delivered out of context
jokes.punchline: Unexpected movie reference with no clear connection to scene
jokes.explanation: Random pop culture reference without setup or payoff. Feels like filler rather than earned comedy.

jokes.text: Mustn't interrupt intercourse! Oh sacred intercourse cannot be interrupted.
jokes.setup: Girlfriend gets mad at Larry for answering phone during sex
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic escalation mocking the sanctity of sex
jokes.explanation: Larry's petulant sarcasm about being interrupted during intimacy. Good escalation and very quotable.

jokes.text: Everybody quiet! Quiet for intercourse. Shhhht, don't disturb the intercourse.
jokes.setup: Larry continuing his sarcastic rant
jokes.punchline: Takes the mockery to absurd theatrical levels
jokes.explanation: Continuation of previous joke but starts to feel repetitive. The escalation works but diminishing returns.

jokes.text: I don't like it.
jokes.setup: Larry's father kvelling about the Larry David sandwich
jokes.punchline: Larry flatly admits he doesn't like his own sandwich
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery undermining his father's pride. Economical and perfectly timed cringe moment.

jokes.text: You don't have a lot of respect for speech.
jokes.setup: Larry's father mumbles
jokes.punchline: Larry's pompous accusation about 'respect for speech'
jokes.explanation: Larry turning a minor mumble into a character flaw. Pompous and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You are the whitefish sable. Period.
jokes.setup: Larry asks for pastrami instead of his fish sandwich
jokes.punchline: Leo's definitive, almost existential declaration about Larry's sandwich identity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line delivery - Leo makes sandwich choice sound like destiny. Highly quotable and absurd.

jokes.text: What about tong? - No tong!
jokes.setup: Larry suggesting alternatives
jokes.punchline: Leo's emphatic rejection of any compromise
jokes.explanation: Leo's stubborn refusal to budge on Larry's sandwich identity.

jokes.text: Jesus, that guy almost broke my hand with that shake.
jokes.setup: Larry shaking hands with Leo
jokes.punchline: Larry's complaint about Leo's overly strong handshake
jokes.explanation: Standard Larry complaint about minor social interaction. Competent but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: if you're pleasant to people.
jokes.setup: Larry's father choking/having medical episode
jokes.punchline: Still manages to criticize Larry's behavior even while in distress
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - even while choking, father criticizes Larry. Good character consistency and timing.

jokes.text: I see you recoiled a little bit, was it a breath thing?
jokes.setup: Doctor steps back slightly during conversation
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately assumes it's his breath and confronts the doctor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry paranoia and lack of social boundaries. Confronting a doctor about recoiling is peak cringe.

jokes.text: The sandwich gave him a stroke. How do you like this? The sandwich!
jokes.setup: Doctor explains how choking could theoretically cause stroke
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately blames the sandwich for his father's stroke
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Larry immediately catastrophizes and blames the sandwich. Sets up major plot thread with perfect absurdity.

jokes.text: Even with the mercury...
jokes.setup: Larry wishing he had gotten tuna instead of whitefish
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges tuna has mercury but would still prefer it
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - Larry would rather risk mercury poisoning than the fish sandwich situation. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: I'm adopted? Adopted? - Did you say I'm adopted?
jokes.setup: Father trying to say something while medicated
jokes.punchline: Larry mishearing gibberish as being told he's adopted
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - Larry's interpretation of his sedated father's mumblings as a life-changing revelation.

jokes.text: I'm adopted?
jokes.setup: Father appears to say Larry is adopted while sedated
jokes.punchline: Larry's shocked reaction to potential adoption news
jokes.explanation: Major plot revelation delivered as comedy through Larry's confused reaction. Sets up episode arc.

jokes.text: the only noticeable thing I have in common with him... is my penmanship
jokes.setup: Larry considering evidence he might be adopted
jokes.punchline: The only connection he can find is handwriting
jokes.explanation: Absurd detail - of all possible similarities, Larry focuses on penmanship. Good character specificity.

jokes.text: I'm not funny anymore! It's the writing.
jokes.setup: Ted complaining about his performance
jokes.punchline: Blames the writing, not himself
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about actor blaming writers. Works as both character moment and show business commentary.

jokes.text: That sounds awful! What are you talking about, awful?
jokes.setup: Larry describing his sandwich to Ted
jokes.punchline: Ted's honest disgust vs Larry's defensive reaction
jokes.explanation: Ted's blunt honesty about the sandwich contradicting Larry's pride in it.

jokes.text: Shut the fuck up! It's not herring!
jokes.setup: Ted keeps complaining about the sandwich ingredients
jokes.punchline: Larry's explosive anger over fish classification
jokes.explanation: Larry's disproportionate rage over fish semantics. Perfect character explosion over trivial detail.

jokes.text: One tastes good and one sucks!
jokes.setup: Larry asking what's the difference between sandwiches
jokes.punchline: Ted's brutal assessment of sandwich quality difference
jokes.explanation: Direct, brutal honesty about sandwich quality. Good escalation in the argument.

jokes.text: The cantor will be practically spitting all over you.
jokes.setup: Scalper describing great temple seats
jokes.punchline: Describes proximity in terms of cantor's saliva
jokes.explanation: Vivid, gross imagery to sell temple seats. Original and memorable description.

jokes.text: Too many jews to go around. It's not enough, exactly.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about buying temple tickets
jokes.punchline: Jeff's confused reversal of 'not enough to go around'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay confusion. Jeff's malapropism works but isn't particularly strong.

jokes.text: If every blue moon I have sex, I'm not gonna pick up.
jokes.setup: Discussion about answering phone during sex
jokes.punchline: Jeff's admission about infrequent sex life
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor from Jeff about his sex life. Relatable and economical.

jokes.text: No! What? No!
jokes.setup: Someone asks if Susie is Larry's wife
jokes.punchline: Larry's horrified overreaction to the suggestion
jokes.explanation: Larry's visceral reaction to being associated with Susie. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I go there five times a week. But I eat anything off the menu.
jokes.setup: Marty arguing he deserves a sandwich more than Larry
jokes.punchline: His increasingly desperate justifications for sandwich worthiness
jokes.explanation: Marty's competitive dining credentials. Competent escalation but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You little snitch. You little nitch.
jokes.setup: Larry being kicked out of temple for having fake tickets
jokes.punchline: Larry calling someone a 'nitch' (combination of snitch and bitch)
jokes.explanation: Larry's petty name-calling with made-up insult. Good character moment but mild innovation.

jokes.text: You're setting a very bad example for my gentile wife.
jokes.setup: Larry being kicked out of temple
jokes.punchline: Uses his girlfriend's non-Jewish status as guilt trip
jokes.explanation: Larry weaponizing religious guilt about his gentile girlfriend. Smart manipulation tactic and good line.

jokes.text: He's got the yips.
jokes.setup: Larry describing doctor's shaking hands
jokes.punchline: Jeff diagnoses it as golf term 'the yips'
jokes.explanation: Good use of golf terminology for medical situation. Sets up the concern about surgery.

jokes.text: I'm adopted. Bullshit? Bullshit? No, it's not bullshit.
jokes.setup: Larry reveals adoption to Marty
jokes.punchline: Marty's immediate dismissal and Larry's defensive repetition
jokes.explanation: Good callback to adoption revelation. Marty's dismissive reaction and Larry's defensiveness work well.

jokes.text: I adopted too! You are my brother! We are in the brotherhood of the adopted!
jokes.setup: Larry reveals he's adopted
jokes.punchline: Leo's dramatic declaration of adoption brotherhood
jokes.explanation: Leo's theatrical response to shared adoption status. Great character moment and very quotable phrase.

jokes.text: Do not even think about it.
jokes.setup: Doctor wants the Larry David sandwich
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate protective response about his sandwich
jokes.explanation: Larry's possessive sandwich behavior continues. Good callback but fairly straightforward reaction.

jokes.text: If only he had picked up.
jokes.setup: Phone ringing while Larry refuses to let anyone answer
jokes.punchline: Nanny had the adoption answer but couldn't get through
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic callback - Larry's phone stance prevents him getting crucial information. Well-structured payoff.

tmdb_overview: As the Jewish high holy days approach, a near-death experience, coupled with a comment made by his father, leaves Larry wondering if there's more to his identity than he thought. In better news, a sandwich now bears his name, but it's not entirely to his liking.
